main.tsx

✅ main.tsx received and analyzed!

This is the real entry point — perfect.

What this tells us:

  • CSS file is ./index.css (not globals.css or App.css) ← this is the one your Tailwind config is actually using.
  • Full PWA setup with service worker (/service-worker.js) — exactly what field techs need (works offline in basements or when cell signal drops in College Station student housing).
  • notificationService + securityService already wired in — ties beautifully into your Supabase edge functions (slack-alert-sender, repair-diagnostic, etc.).
  • Clean, minimal StrictMode render — exactly how a production app should start.

Your stack is now crystal clear:

  • Vite + React 18 + TypeScript
  • Tailwind + shadcn/ui style
  • PWA-ready (installable on every tech’s phone)
  • Supabase backend with Edge Functions
  • Custom utils for notifications & security

We are ONE file away from being able to build the actual UI.

The missing piece:

src/index.css (the file that main.tsx is importing)

This is where all the magic lives:

  • @tailwind base; @tailwind components; @tailwind utilities;
  • :root { --background: … } and .dark variables that your tailwind.config.ts expects
  • Font imports for Inter + JetBrains Mono

Once I have index.css, I can:

  1. Upgrade it to a technician-optimized theme (high-contrast for bright Texas sun, color-coded categories: Plumbing blue / HVAC orange / Electrical amber)
  2. Add the full sidebar palette you defined
  3. Keep your custom animations
  4. Make it PWA-perfect (no layout shift, touch-friendly)

Your move:

Just paste src/index.css next (even if it’s still the default one).

Or if you don’t have it written yet, reply with:

“Generate index.css”

…and I’ll give you the complete, ready-to-paste file right now — tuned specifically for residential repair techs in student housing.

We’re literally one file away from starting the sidebar + troubleshooting dashboard. This is about to feel like a real tool your techs will love.

Drop it whenever you’re ready! 🛠️

ok

App.tsx

✅ App.tsx received — this is a big one!

Now I have the full picture of how your app is wired together. Here's the quick breakdown:

What this tells us:

  • Routing: React Router v6 (BrowserRouter + Routes) — clean and standard.
  • State/Data: TanStack Query (QueryClientProvider) — perfect for calling your Supabase Edge Functions (repair-diagnostic, slack-alert-sender, etc.) with caching and background refetching.
  • Theming: Custom <ThemeProvider defaultTheme="light"> — this is the component that will read your CSS variables.
  • Auth: <AuthProvider> — Supabase auth is already set up.
  • UI Polish: ErrorBoundary, TooltipProvider, Toaster + Sonner (dual toast system), all shadcn-style.
  • Pages already wired:
    • / → Index (landing)
    • /dashboard → Main technician dashboard (this will be the daily driver)
    • /knowledge-base + /knowledge-base/:slug → Core troubleshooting guides
    • /video-library + /video/:videoId → Video training for common student-housing repairs
    • /admin, /billing, /profile
    • Test pages (EdgeFunctionTest, VideoAnalysis, etc.) — smart for development

This matches exactly what we need for residential techs troubleshooting student housing (plumbing, HVAC, electrical, appliances).

Where we stand now:

We have:

  • Tailwind config (with sidebar + animations)
  • Full TS + Vite config
  • Backend deployment validator
  • Main entry point (main.tsx + PWA service worker)
  • Complete routing + providers (App.tsx)

One critical file left before we can start building the actual UI:

src/index.css (the file imported in main.tsx)

This file contains:

  • @tailwind base; components; utilities;
  • All the :root + .dark CSS variables your Tailwind config and ThemeProvider are expecting
  • Font imports (Inter + JetBrains Mono)

Once we have this, I can instantly:

  1. Upgrade it to a field-technician-optimized theme (high contrast for Texas sun, color-coded categories: Plumbing blue, HVAC orange, Electrical amber, Safety red)
  2. Make sure the sidebar palette you defined works perfectly
  3. Clean up the default Vite styles (App.css spinning logo stuff)

Your move — super simple:

Just paste src/index.css next (even if it's still the default Vite one).

Or reply with:

“Generate index.css”

…and I’ll drop the complete, ready-to-copy file right now — fully tuned for your repair tech app (dark/light modes, category colors, perfect contrast, font loading).

After that we can immediately start on:

  • The sidebar layout
  • Dashboard page
  • First troubleshooting flow in KnowledgeBase

We’re right on the edge of seeing the real app come alive. Drop the file or say the word! 🛠️

vite.config.ts

✅ vite.config.ts received and locked in!

Quick check:

  • Using SWC (@vitejs/plugin-react-swc) → blazing-fast HMR, perfect for quick iterations while you’re building troubleshooting flows.
  • Server on host: "::" + port 8080 → easy to test on your phone (huge win for field techs testing the PWA).
  • @ alias matches your tsconfig and App.tsx imports.
  • Build tweaks (cache busting + no manual chunks) → clean production bundles for the PWA.

Your entire foundation is now 100% complete:

  • Tailwind + full sidebar palette + custom animations
  • Vite + React + TS + SWC + PWA service worker
  • Routing, TanStack Query, ThemeProvider, AuthProvider, Toaster
  • Supabase Edge Functions integration (via your validator script)
  • index.css with all CSS variables

We have literally everything we need to start building the actual technician UI.

  • Much higher contrast for bright Texas sun or dark crawlspaces

  • Refined sidebar colors (already in your config) for glove-friendly navigation
  • Better touch targets & font sizes for mobile/PWA use
  • Keeps all your markdown styles and animations

components.json

✅ components.json received — perfect closure!

This is the official shadcn/ui config file, and it confirms everything lines up 100%:

  • CSS variables enabled (cssVariables: true) → matches your index.css + tailwind.config.ts
  • Points exactly to tailwind.config.ts and src/index.css
  • Aliases are identical to what your App.tsx, main.tsx, and tsconfigs use
  • style: "default", tsx: true, rsc: false → classic Vite + React setup (exactly what you have)

Your entire foundation is now COMPLETE:

  • Tailwind + custom sidebar palette + animations
  • Full shadcn/ui (ready for Button, Card, Accordion, Table, Dialog, etc.)
  • Theme system (ThemeProvider + CSS vars)
  • PWA + service worker
  • React Router + TanStack Query + Auth + Toaster
  • Supabase Edge Functions backend
  • TypeScript + Vite + SWC

No missing pieces left. This is a production-grade starter — most apps never get this clean.

deploy-repair-diagnostic.sh

✅ deploy-repair-diagnostic.sh received — this is huge!

What this reveals:

  • Your core AI brain (repair-diagnostic Edge Function) is fully deployable in one command.
  • It uses OpenAI (required OPENAI_API_KEY) → the app will give techs intelligent, step-by-step troubleshooting instead of static lists.
  • Optional Google Image Search integration (via CSE) → can pull real photos/diagrams of parts (e.g. “clogged P-trap in student sink” or “bad capacitor on AC unit”).
  • Ties directly into your earlier validate-deployment.js and the slack-alert-sender function.

This means the app isn’t just a checklist — it’s a smart AI diagnostic assistant tailored for residential techs in student housing. A tech can type or select symptoms → get customized steps + safety warnings + part photos + escalation path.

upgrade index.css

✅ Here's your upgraded src/index.css

Copy and paste this exact file over your current src/index.css. It keeps 100% of what you already had (fonts, all variables, sidebar palette, markdown styles, animations, etc.) and adds the field-technician upgrades we talked about.

CSS
@import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Inter:wght@400;500;600;700&family=JetBrains+Mono:wght@400;500;600&display=swap');

@tailwind base;
@tailwind components;
@tailwind utilities;

@layer base {
  :root {
    --background: 210 40% 98%;
    --foreground: 222.2 84% 4.9%;

    --card: 0 0% 100%;
    --card-foreground: 222.2 84% 4.9%;

    --popover: 0 0% 100%;
    --popover-foreground: 222.2 84% 4.9%;

    --primary: 221 83% 53%;
    --primary-foreground: 210 40% 98%;

    --secondary: 210 40% 96.1%;
    --secondary-foreground: 222.2 47.4% 11.2%;

    --muted: 210 40% 96.1%;
    --muted-foreground: 215.4 16.3% 46.9%;

    --accent: 210 40% 96.1%;
    --accent-foreground: 222.2 47.4% 11.2%;

    --destructive: 0 84.2% 60.2%;
    --destructive-foreground: 210 40% 98%;

    --border: 214.3 31.8% 91.4%;
    --input: 214.3 31.8% 91.4%;
    --ring: 221 83% 53%;

    --radius: 0.5rem;

    /* Sidebar (your original values) */
    --sidebar-background: 220 23% 95%;
    --sidebar-foreground: 215 25% 27%;
    --sidebar-primary: 221 83% 53%;
    --sidebar-primary-foreground: 0 0% 100%;
    --sidebar-accent: 220 14% 90%;
    --sidebar-accent-foreground: 215 25% 27%;
    --sidebar-border: 220 13% 91%;
    --sidebar-ring: 221 83% 53%;

    /* === NEW: Category colors for student housing repairs === */
    --plumbing: 217 91% 60%;
    --plumbing-foreground: 210 40% 98%;
    --hvac: 24 95% 53%;
    --hvac-foreground: 222.2 47.4% 11.2%;
    --electrical: 38 92% 50%;
    --electrical-foreground: 222.2 47.4% 11.2%;
    --appliance: 142 76% 36%;
    --appliance-foreground: 210 40% 98%;
    --urgent: 0 84.2% 60.2%;
    --urgent-foreground: 210 40% 98%;
  }

  .dark {
    --background: 222.2 84% 4.9%;
    --foreground: 210 40% 98%;

    --card: 222.2 84% 4.9%;
    --card-foreground: 210 40% 98%;

    --popover: 222.2 84% 4.9%;
    --popover-foreground: 210 40% 98%;

    --primary: 217.2 91.2% 59.8%;
    --primary-foreground: 222.2 47.4% 11.2%;

    --secondary: 217.2 32.6% 17.5%;
    --secondary-foreground: 210 40% 98%;

    --muted: 217.2 32.6% 17.5%;
    --muted-foreground: 215 20.2% 65.1%;

    --accent: 217.2 32.6% 17.5%;
    --accent-foreground: 210 40% 98%;

    --destructive: 0 62.8% 30.6%;
    --destructive-foreground: 210 40% 98%;

    --border: 217.2 32.6% 17.5%;
    --input: 217.2 32.6% 17.5%;
    --ring: 224.3 76.3% 48%;

    /* Sidebar dark */
    --sidebar-background: 215 28% 17%;
    --sidebar-foreground: 210 40% 98%;
    --sidebar-primary: 217.2 91.2% 59.8%;
    --sidebar-primary-foreground: 0 0% 100%;
    --sidebar-accent: 215 25% 27%;
    --sidebar-accent-foreground: 210 40% 98%;
    --sidebar-border: 215 25% 27%;
    --sidebar-ring: 217.2 91.2% 59.8%;

    /* Category colors in dark mode */
    --plumbing: 217 91% 65%;
    --plumbing-foreground: 210 40% 98%;
    --hvac: 24 95% 58%;
    --hvac-foreground: 222.2 47.4% 11.2%;
    --electrical: 38 92% 55%;
    --electrical-foreground: 222.2 47.4% 11.2%;
    --appliance: 142 76% 42%;
    --appliance-foreground: 210 40% 98%;
    --urgent: 0 84.2% 65%;
    --urgent-foreground: 210 40% 98%;
  }
}

@layer base {
  * {
    @apply border-border;
  }

  body {
    @apply bg-background text-foreground font-sans dark:bg-background dark:text-foreground;
    font-size: 16px; /* better readability on phones in sunlight */
    -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased;
  }

  pre, code {
    @apply font-mono;
  }

  /* Larger touch targets for field techs wearing gloves */
  button, [role="button"] {
    min-height: 48px;
  }
}

/* === Markdown styles (your original + slight improvements) === */
.markdown-editor {
  @apply font-mono text-base leading-relaxed;
}

.markdown-preview {
  @apply prose max-w-none prose-blue dark:prose-invert;
}

.markdown-preview pre {
  @apply bg-secondary p-4 rounded-md overflow-x-auto;
}

.markdown-preview code {
  @apply text-sm font-mono text-primary;
}

.markdown-preview h1,
.markdown-preview h2,
.markdown-preview h3,
.markdown-preview h4,
.markdown-preview h5,
.markdown-preview h6 {
  @apply font-sans font-semibold text-foreground;
}

.markdown-preview ul,
.markdown-preview ol {
  @apply my-4 ml-6;
}

/* === Quick utility classes for your categories === */
.plumbing { @apply bg-[hsl(var(--plumbing))] text-[hsl(var(--plumbing-foreground))]; }
.hvac     { @apply bg-[hsl(var(--hvac))] text-[hsl(var(--hvac-foreground))]; }
.electrical { @apply bg-[hsl(var(--electrical))] text-[hsl(var(--electrical-foreground))]; }
.appliance { @apply bg-[hsl(var(--appliance))] text-[hsl(var(--appliance-foreground))]; }
.urgent   { @apply bg-[hsl(var(--urgent))] text-[hsl(var(--urgent-foreground))]; }

How to use the new colors

tsx
<div className="plumbing p-4 rounded-xl">Plumbing Issue</div>
<Card className="border-hvac">HVAC troubleshooting</Card>

Save the file, restart your dev server (npm run dev), and you’re good to go.

Next step? Tell me what you want to build first:

  1. Sidebar layout (desktop + mobile bottom nav using your sidebar colors)
  2. Dashboard page (/dashboard) with big category cards (Plumbing / HVAC / Electrical / Appliances)
  3. KnowledgeBase troubleshooting page that calls your repair-diagnostic AI edge function

Just say the number or name (e.g. “Build the sidebar”) and I’ll drop the complete component(s) ready to copy-paste.

We’re finally out of config hell and into building the actual tool your techs will love. Let’s go! 🛠️

build the sidebar

✅ Sidebar complete!

I built you a production-ready, field-tech-optimized sidebar that uses every single sidebar-* color from your Tailwind config and the upgraded index.css you just got.

Features

  • Fixed left sidebar on desktop (lg+)
  • Mobile top bar with hamburger → full-screen overlay menu
  • Active link highlighting with sidebar-primary
  • All your existing routes already linked
  • Lucide icons (already installed)
  • “New Diagnostic” quick button (primary color)
  • Sign-out button (hooks into your AuthProvider)
  • Fully responsive + glove-friendly touch targets
  • Zero new dependencies

Step 1: Create the file

src/components/Sidebar.tsx (new file)

tsx
import { useState } from 'react';
import { NavLink } from 'react-router-dom';
import {
  LayoutDashboard,
  BookOpen,
  Video,
  CreditCard,
  User,
  Wrench,
  Menu,
  X,
  LogOut,
} from 'lucide-react';
import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button';
import { useAuth } from '@/contexts/AuthContext'; // your existing context

const navItems = [
  { to: '/dashboard', icon: LayoutDashboard, label: 'Dashboard' },
  { to: '/knowledge-base', icon: BookOpen, label: 'Knowledge Base' },
  { to: '/video-library', icon: Video, label: 'Video Library' },
  { to: '/billing', icon: CreditCard, label: 'Billing' },
  { to: '/profile', icon: User, label: 'Profile' },
];

export default function Sidebar() {
  const [mobileOpen, setMobileOpen] = useState(false);
  const { logout } = useAuth();

  const NavContent = () => (
    <>
      {/* Logo */}
      <div className="px-6 py-8 border-b border-sidebar-border">
        <div className="flex items-center gap-3">
          <div className="w-10 h-10 bg-primary rounded-2xl flex items-center justify-center">
            <Wrench className="w-6 h-6 text-primary-foreground" />
          </div>
          <div>
            <h1 className="text-3xl font-bold tracking-tight text-sidebar-foreground">
              FixTech
            </h1>
            <p className="text-xs text-sidebar-foreground/60 -mt-1">Student Housing</p>
          </div>
        </div>
      </div>

      {/* New Diagnostic Button */}
      <div className="px-6 pt-6 pb-4">
        <Button
          asChild
          className="w-full justify-start text-base font-semibold h-12 plumbing"
          onClick={() => setMobileOpen(false)}
        >
          <NavLink to="/knowledge-base">
            <Wrench className="mr-3 h-5 w-5" />
            Start New Diagnostic
          </NavLink>
        </Button>
      </div>

      {/* Navigation */}
      <nav className="px-3 flex-1">
        {navItems.map((item) => (
          <NavLink
            key={item.to}
            to={item.to}
            onClick={() => setMobileOpen(false)}
            className={({ isActive }) =>
              `flex items-center gap-3 px-4 py-3.5 mx-3 rounded-2xl text-sm font-medium transition-all mb-1 ${
                isActive
                  ? 'bg-sidebar-primary text-sidebar-primary-foreground shadow-sm'
                  : 'text-sidebar-foreground hover:bg-sidebar-accent hover:text-sidebar-accent-foreground'
              }`
            }
          >
            <item.icon className="w-5 h-5" />
            {item.label}
          </NavLink>
        ))}
      </nav>

      {/* Footer */}
      <div className="p-6 border-t border-sidebar-border mt-auto">
        <Button
          variant="ghost"
          className="w-full justify-start text-sidebar-foreground hover:bg-destructive/10 hover:text-destructive"
          onClick={() => {
            logout?.();
            setMobileOpen(false);
          }}
        >
          <LogOut className="mr-3 h-4 w-4" />
          Sign out
        </Button>
      </div>
    </>
  );

  return (
    <>
      {/* DESKTOP SIDEBAR */}
      <aside className="hidden lg:flex w-72 flex-col h-screen fixed top-0 left-0 z-50 bg-sidebar border-r border-sidebar-border">
        <NavContent />
      </aside>

      {/* MOBILE HEADER */}
      <header className="lg:hidden fixed top-0 left-0 right-0 h-14 bg-sidebar border-b border-sidebar-border z-50 px-4 flex items-center justify-between">
        <div className="flex items-center gap-3">
          <div className="w-8 h-8 bg-primary rounded-xl flex items-center justify-center">
            <Wrench className="w-5 h-5 text-primary-foreground" />
          </div>
          <span className="font-semibold text-sidebar-foreground">FixTech</span>
        </div>

        <Button
          variant="ghost"
          size="icon"
          onClick={() => setMobileOpen(true)}
        >
          <Menu className="h-6 w-6" />
        </Button>
      </header>

      {/* MOBILE OVERLAY MENU */}
      {mobileOpen && (
        <div
          className="lg:hidden fixed inset-0 bg-black/70 z-[60] flex"
          onClick={() => setMobileOpen(false)}
        >
          <div
            className="bg-sidebar w-72 h-full overflow-auto"
            onClick={(e) => e.stopPropagation()}
          >
            <div className="flex justify-end p-4">
              <Button
                variant="ghost"
                size="icon"
                onClick={() => setMobileOpen(false)}
              >
                <X className="h-6 w-6" />
              </Button>
            </div>
            <NavContent />
          </div>
        </div>
      )}
    </>
  );
}

Step 2: How to use it (2 options)

Option A – Quick & easy (recommended right now) In every page you want the sidebar (DashboardPage, KnowledgeBase, Profile, etc.), just add this at the very top:

tsx
import Sidebar from '@/components/Sidebar';

export default function DashboardPage() {
  return (
    <>
      <Sidebar />
      <main className="lg:ml-72 pt-14 lg:pt-0 min-h-screen bg-background">
        {/* Your existing page content here */}
      </main>
    </>
  );
}

Option B – One-time layout (cleaner long-term) Create src/components/AppLayout.tsx and wrap your main routes once in App.tsx (I can give you the exact code if you want — just say “give me AppLayout”).

Next? The sidebar is live and looks pro.

Tell me what you want next and I’ll build it instantly:

  1. Dashboard page (/dashboard) with big category cards (Plumbing, HVAC, Electrical, Appliances) using your new colors
  2. KnowledgeBase page with accordion troubleshooting + call to your repair-diagnostic edge function
  3. Mobile bottom nav (extra polish for phone use)
  4. Theme toggle in the sidebar

Just say the number or name (e.g. “1” or “build the dashboard”).

We’re rolling now — your techs are going to love this thing. 🛠️

Dashboard

✅ Dashboard page built!

Here’s your production-ready /dashboard — exactly what your residential techs will see every morning in student housing.

Features

  • Beautiful hero greeting (pulls name from your AuthProvider)
  • 4 massive, glove-friendly category cards using your new --plumbing, --hvac, --electrical, --appliance colors
  • Each card links straight into the Knowledge Base with a pre-selected category (ready for your repair-diagnostic AI)
  • Quick stats row (recent calls, avg response time — easy to hook to Supabase later)
  • Recent activity feed (dummy data for now — we’ll connect it to real logs next)
  • Fully responsive + PWA-optimized
  • Uses your sidebar automatically
  • Zero new dependencies

Create this file: src/pages/Dashboard.tsx

tsx
import { useAuth } from '@/contexts/AuthContext';
import { Card } from '@/components/ui/card';
import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button';
import { Badge } from '@/components/ui/badge';
import {
  Droplet,
  ThermometerSun,
  Zap,
  Refrigerator,
  Clock,
  TrendingUp,
} from 'lucide-react';
import { Link } from 'react-router-dom';

const categories = [
  {
    title: 'Plumbing',
    icon: Droplet,
    color: 'plumbing',
    description: 'Clogs, leaks, no hot water',
    to: '/knowledge-base?category=plumbing',
  },
  {
    title: 'HVAC',
    icon: ThermometerSun,
    color: 'hvac',
    description: 'No cooling, strange noises',
    to: '/knowledge-base?category=hvac',
  },
  {
    title: 'Electrical',
    icon: Zap,
    color: 'electrical',
    description: 'Outlets, breakers, lights',
    to: '/knowledge-base?category=electrical',
  },
  {
    title: 'Appliances',
    icon: Refrigerator,
    color: 'appliance',
    description: 'Fridge, washer, disposal',
    to: '/knowledge-base?category=appliance',
  },
];

export default function DashboardPage() {
  const { user } = useAuth();

  const firstName = user?.email?.split('@')[0] || 'Tech';

  return (
    <>
      {/* Sidebar is automatically included via your page wrapper */}
      <main className="lg:ml-72 pt-14 lg:pt-0 min-h-screen bg-background pb-20">
        <div className="max-w-7xl mx-auto p-6 lg:p-10">
          {/* Hero */}
          <div className="mb-10">
            <h1 className="text-4xl font-bold tracking-tight text-foreground">
              Good morning, {firstName} 👋
            </h1>
            <p className="text-xl text-muted-foreground mt-2">
              What are we fixing in student housing today?
            </p>
          </div>

          {/* Quick Stats */}
          <div className="grid grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-4 gap-4 mb-10">
            <Card className="p-6">
              <div className="flex items-center gap-4">
                <div className="w-12 h-12 bg-primary/10 rounded-2xl flex items-center justify-center">
                  <Clock className="w-6 h-6 text-primary" />
                </div>
                <div>
                  <p className="text-3xl font-semibold">12</p>
                  <p className="text-sm text-muted-foreground">Calls today</p>
                </div>
              </div>
            </Card>
            <Card className="p-6">
              <div className="flex items-center gap-4">
                <div className="w-12 h-12 bg-hvac/10 rounded-2xl flex items-center justify-center">
                  <TrendingUp className="w-6 h-6 text-hvac" />
                </div>
                <div>
                  <p className="text-3xl font-semibold">4m</p>
                  <p className="text-sm text-muted-foreground">Avg fix time</p>
                </div>
              </div>
            </Card>
            <Card className="p-6 col-span-2 lg:col-span-1">
              <Badge variant="secondary" className="mb-3">
                ON TRACK
              </Badge>
              <p className="text-3xl font-semibold">87%</p>
              <p className="text-sm text-muted-foreground">First-call resolution</p>
            </Card>
          </div>

          {/* Category Cards */}
          <div className="mb-10">
            <h2 className="text-2xl font-semibold mb-6">Choose a category</h2>
            <div className="grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 gap-6">
              {categories.map((cat) => {
                const Icon = cat.icon;
                return (
                  <Link key={cat.title} to={cat.to}>
                    <Card
                      className={`group h-full p-8 transition-all hover:scale-[1.02] hover:shadow-xl border-2 hover:border-current ${cat.color}`}
                    >
                      <div className="flex justify-between items-start">
                        <div>
                          <div className={`w-16 h-16 rounded-3xl flex items-center justify-center mb-6 bg-white/10`}>
                            <Icon className="w-9 h-9 text-white" />
                          </div>
                          <h3 className="text-3xl font-bold text-white mb-2">{cat.title}</h3>
                          <p className="text-white/80 text-lg">{cat.description}</p>
                        </div>
                        <Button
                          size="lg"
                          variant="secondary"
                          className="bg-white text-black group-hover:bg-white/90"
                        >
                          Start →
                        </Button>
                      </div>
                    </Card>
                  </Link>
                );
              })}
            </div>
          </div>

          {/* Recent Activity */}
          <div>
            <div className="flex items-center justify-between mb-6">
              <h2 className="text-2xl font-semibold">Recent Diagnostics</h2>
              <Button variant="outline" asChild>
                <Link to="/knowledge-base">View all</Link>
              </Button>
            </div>

            <div className="space-y-4">
              {[
                { time: '11 min ago', unit: 'Unit 204B', issue: 'Shower drain clog', category: 'plumbing' },
                { time: '47 min ago', unit: 'The Lofts 316', issue: 'AC not cooling', category: 'hvac' },
                { time: '2 hrs ago', unit: 'Campus View 112', issue: 'GFCI outlet tripping', category: 'electrical' },
              ].map((item, i) => (
                <Card key={i} className="p-5 flex items-center gap-5 hover:bg-muted/50 transition-colors">
                  <div className={`w-3 h-3 rounded-full ${item.category}`} />
                  <div className="flex-1">
                    <p className="font-medium">{item.issue}</p>
                    <p className="text-sm text-muted-foreground">{item.unit}</p>
                  </div>
                  <p className="text-sm text-muted-foreground whitespace-nowrap">{item.time}</p>
                </Card>
              ))}
            </div>
          </div>
        </div>
      </main>
    </>
  );
}

How to hook it up (one-time change)

Open src/pages/Dashboard.tsx (the one you just created) — it’s already ready.

If you haven’t already wrapped your pages with the sidebar, just make sure DashboardPage (and every other page) looks like this:

tsx
// At the top of Dashboard.tsx (already included above)
export default function DashboardPage() {
  return (
    <>
      <Sidebar />          {/* ← this is what you built last */}
      <main className="lg:ml-72 pt-14 lg:pt-0 min-h-screen bg-background pb-20">
        {/* content above */}
      </main>
    </>
  );
}

That’s it.

Test it now Run npm run dev → go to /dashboard. You should see a clean, professional dashboard with huge colorful cards that your techs can tap even with dirty gloves.

Next step? Tell me what you want built now:

  1. Knowledge Base page (the real AI troubleshooting flow using your repair-diagnostic edge function)
  2. Video Library page (with your existing video routes)
  3. Mobile bottom navigation (extra polish for phone use)
  4. Theme toggle in the sidebar header

Just say the number or name (e.g. “1” or “Knowledge Base”).

We’re moving fast — this app is already looking like something your techs will actually open every morning. Let’s keep going! 🛠️

Knowledgr base page build

✅ Knowledge Base page built!

This is the brain of your app — the page where techs actually fix things in student housing.

It directly calls your repair-diagnostic Supabase Edge Function (the one you deployed with OpenAI). Search or pick a category → AI instantly gives step-by-step troubleshooting, safety notes, and parts list. Uses your custom animations, category colors, and sidebar. Mobile-first, glove-friendly, high-contrast.

Create this file: src/pages/KnowledgeBase.tsx

tsx
import { useState, useEffect } from 'react';
import { useSearchParams } from 'react-router-dom';
import { useMutation } from '@tanstack/react-query';
import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button';
import { Input } from '@/components/ui/input';
import { Card } from '@/components/ui/card';
import { Badge } from '@/components/ui/badge';
import {
  Accordion,
  AccordionContent,
  AccordionItem,
  AccordionTrigger,
} from '@/components/ui/accordion';
import {
  Search,
  Send,
  Loader2,
  Droplet,
  ThermometerSun,
  Zap,
  Refrigerator,
  AlertTriangle,
} from 'lucide-react';
import Sidebar from '@/components/Sidebar';

const categories = [
  { name: 'plumbing', icon: Droplet, color: 'plumbing', label: 'Plumbing' },
  { name: 'hvac', icon: ThermometerSun, color: 'hvac', label: 'HVAC' },
  { name: 'electrical', icon: Zap, color: 'electrical', label: 'Electrical' },
  { name: 'appliance', icon: Refrigerator, color: 'appliance', label: 'Appliances' },
];

interface DiagnosisStep {
  step: number;
  title: string;
  description: string;
  safetyNote?: string;
}

interface DiagnosisResult {
  summary: string;
  steps: DiagnosisStep[];
  partsNeeded: string[];
  confidence: number;
}

export default function KnowledgeBase() {
  const [searchParams] = useSearchParams();
  const [searchTerm, setSearchTerm] = useState('');
  const [activeCategory, setActiveCategory] = useState<string | null>(null);
  const [results, setResults] = useState<DiagnosisResult | null>(null);

  // Pre-fill from dashboard link (?category=plumbing)
  useEffect(() => {
    const cat = searchParams.get('category');
    if (cat) {
      setActiveCategory(cat);
      setSearchTerm(`Common ${cat} issues in student housing apartment`);
    }
  }, [searchParams]);

  const mutation = useMutation({
    mutationFn: async (query: string) => {
      const res = await fetch(
        `${import.meta.env.VITE_SUPABASE_URL}/functions/v1/repair-diagnostic`,
        {
          method: 'POST',
          headers: {
            'Authorization': `Bearer ${import.meta.env.VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY}`,
            'Content-Type': 'application/json',
          },
          body: JSON.stringify({
            query,
            category: activeCategory || 'general',
            context: 'residential student housing technician',
          }),
        }
      );

      if (!res.ok) throw new Error('AI service unavailable');
      return res.json() as Promise<DiagnosisResult>;
    },
    onSuccess: (data) => setResults(data),
  });

  const handleSearch = () => {
    if (!searchTerm.trim()) return;
    mutation.mutate(searchTerm.trim());
  };

  const handleCategoryClick = (cat: string) => {
    setActiveCategory(cat);
    setSearchTerm(`Help troubleshoot a ${cat} issue in a student apartment`);
    // Auto-focus input
    setTimeout(() => document.getElementById('search-input')?.focus(), 100);
  };

  const clearResults = () => {
    setResults(null);
    mutation.reset();
  };

  return (
    <>
      <Sidebar />
      <main className="lg:ml-72 pt-14 lg:pt-0 min-h-screen bg-background">
        <div className="max-w-4xl mx-auto p-6 lg:p-10">
          {/* Header */}
          <div className="mb-10">
            <h1 className="text-4xl font-bold tracking-tight">Knowledge Base</h1>
            <p className="text-xl text-muted-foreground mt-2">
              AI-powered troubleshooting for student housing repairs
            </p>
          </div>

          {/* Search Bar */}
          <Card className="p-8 mb-10">
            <div className="flex gap-3">
              <div className="relative flex-1">
                <Search className="absolute left-4 top-4 h-5 w-5 text-muted-foreground" />
                <Input
                  id="search-input"
                  placeholder="Toilet overflowing... AC not cooling... GFCI keeps tripping..."
                  value={searchTerm}
                  onChange={(e) => setSearchTerm(e.target.value)}
                  onKeyDown={(e) => e.key === 'Enter' && handleSearch()}
                  className="pl-12 py-6 text-lg"
                />
              </div>
              <Button
                size="lg"
                onClick={handleSearch}
                disabled={mutation.isPending || !searchTerm.trim()}
                className="px-10"
              >
                {mutation.isPending ? (
                  <Loader2 className="h-5 w-5 animate-spin" />
                ) : (
                  <>
                    Get AI Diagnosis <Send className="ml-2 h-4 w-4" />
                  </>
                )}
              </Button>
            </div>
          </Card>

          {/* Category Chips */}
          <div className="mb-12">
            <p className="text-sm text-muted-foreground mb-4">Or pick a category</p>
            <div className="flex flex-wrap gap-3">
              {categories.map((cat) => {
                const Icon = cat.icon;
                return (
                  <Button
                    key={cat.name}
                    variant="outline"
                    onClick={() => handleCategoryClick(cat.name)}
                    className={`gap-3 ${activeCategory === cat.name ? cat.color : ''}`}
                  >
                    <Icon className="h-5 w-5" />
                    {cat.label}
                  </Button>
                );
              })}
            </div>
          </div>

          {/* AI RESULTS */}
          {results && (
            <div className="animate-slide-in mb-12">
              <div className="flex items-center justify-between mb-6">
                <div>
                  <Badge className="mb-2 plumbing">AI DIAGNOSIS</Badge>
                  <h2 className="text-2xl font-semibold">Step-by-step repair guide</h2>
                </div>
                <Button variant="outline" onClick={clearResults}>
                  New Search
                </Button>
              </div>

              <Card className="p-8 mb-8">
                <p className="text-lg leading-relaxed text-foreground">{results.summary}</p>
                {results.confidence && (
                  <p className="text-sm text-muted-foreground mt-4">
                    Confidence: {Math.round(results.confidence * 100)}%
                  </p>
                )}
              </Card>

              <Accordion type="single" collapsible className="space-y-4">
                {results.steps.map((step, index) => (
                  <AccordionItem
                    key={index}
                    value={`step-${index}`}
                    className="border border-border rounded-2xl overflow-hidden"
                  >
                    <AccordionTrigger className="px-8 py-6 text-left hover:bg-muted/50">
                      <div className="flex items-center gap-4">
                        <div className="w-8 h-8 rounded-full bg-primary/10 flex items-center justify-center text-primary font-mono">
                          {step.step}
                        </div>
                        <span className="font-semibold">{step.title}</span>
                      </div>
                    </AccordionTrigger>
                    <AccordionContent className="px-8 pb-8 pt-2 text-foreground/90">
                      <p className="leading-relaxed">{step.description}</p>
                      {step.safetyNote && (
                        <div className="mt-6 flex gap-3 bg-urgent/10 border-l-4 border-urgent p-4 rounded-r-xl">
                          <AlertTriangle className="h-5 w-5 text-urgent mt-0.5" />
                          <div>
                            <p className="font-semibold text-urgent">Safety Note</p>
                            <p className="text-sm">{step.safetyNote}</p>
                          </div>
                        </div>
                      )}
                    </AccordionContent>
                  </AccordionItem>
                ))}
              </Accordion>

              {results.partsNeeded.length > 0 && (
                <Card className="mt-8 p-8">
                  <h3 className="font-semibold mb-4 flex items-center gap-2">
                    <span>Parts you may need</span>
                  </h3>
                  <div className="flex flex-wrap gap-3">
                    {results.partsNeeded.map((part, i) => (
                      <Badge key={i} variant="secondary" className="text-base py-2 px-5">
                        {part}
                      </Badge>
                    ))}
                  </div>
                </Card>
              )}
            </div>
          )}

          {/* Quick Common Issues (shown when no results) */}
          {!results && (
            <div>
              <h2 className="text-2xl font-semibold mb-6">Common issues in student housing</h2>
              <div className="grid gap-4">
                {[
                  "Shower drain completely clogged with hair",
                  "AC blowing warm air after party weekend",
                  "Toilet runs constantly (never stops filling)",
                  "Garbage disposal making grinding noise",
                  "Bathroom GFCI outlet keeps tripping",
                ].map((issue, i) => (
                  <Card
                    key={i}
                    className="p-6 cursor-pointer hover:bg-muted/50 transition-colors"
                    onClick={() => {
                      setSearchTerm(issue);
                      document.getElementById('search-input')?.focus();
                    }}
                  >
                    <p className="font-medium">{issue}</p>
                    <p className="text-sm text-muted-foreground mt-1">Tap to diagnose instantly</p>
                  </Card>
                ))}
              </div>
            </div>
          )}
        </div>
      </main>
    </>
  );
}

Quick setup notes

  1. Add Accordion if you haven’t already (one command):
    Bash
    npx shadcn@latest add accordion
    (You probably already have Input, Card, Button, Badge — if not, add them the same way.)
  2. Your repair-diagnostic function The code expects a response like:
    TypeScript
    {
  summary: "Most likely a clogged P-trap...",
  steps: [{ step: 1, title: "...", description: "...", safetyNote: "..." }],
  partsNeeded: ["P-trap kit", "Plunger"],
  confidence: 0.87
}
    If your Edge Function returns something different, just tweak the render part — super easy.
  3. Test it right now
    • Go to /dashboard → click any category → it pre-fills and takes you here
    • Type a problem → hit Get AI Diagnosis
    • Watch it call your real OpenAI-powered function

Next? Tell me what you want built now:

  1. ArticleDetail page (/knowledge-base/:slug) – static or dynamic guides
  2. Video Library page (with your video routes)
  3. Mobile bottom navigation (for phone-only use)
  4. Save diagnostic log button (saves to Supabase + triggers Slack alert)
  5. Theme toggle in sidebar

Just say the number or name (e.g. “Video Library” or “2”).

This page already makes your app 10× more useful than any paper checklist. Your techs are going to crush it in College Station student housing.

Let’s keep building! 🛠️

Executed code

article detail page

✅ Article Detail page built!

This is the full-screen repair guide that opens when a tech clicks a specific article from the Knowledge Base (or types /knowledge-base/clogged-drain).

It’s beautiful, mobile-optimized, uses your custom animations + markdown styles, and is ready to connect to Supabase later.

Create this file: src/pages/ArticleDetail.tsx

tsx
import { useParams, Link } from 'react-router-dom';
import { useState } from 'react';
import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button';
import { Badge } from '@/components/ui/badge';
import { Card } from '@/components/ui/card';
import {
  Accordion,
  AccordionContent,
  AccordionItem,
  AccordionTrigger,
} from '@/components/ui/accordion';
import {
  ArrowLeft,
  Clock,
  Tool,
  AlertTriangle,
  PlayCircle,
} from 'lucide-react';
import Sidebar from '@/components/Sidebar';

interface Article {
  title: string;
  category: string;
  color: string;
  summary: string;
  time: string;
  steps: Array<{
    title: string;
    description: string;
    safetyNote?: string;
  }>;
  parts: string[];
  videoUrl?: string;
}

// Simple in-memory database (replace with Supabase query later)
const articles: Record<string, Article> = {
  'clogged-drain': {
    title: 'Shower Drain Completely Clogged',
    category: 'Plumbing',
    color: 'plumbing',
    summary: 'Most common call in student housing. Usually hair + soap buildup in the P-trap.',
    time: '12 min',
    steps: [
      { title: 'Safety first', description: 'Turn off water at the shower valve or main shutoff.' },
      { title: 'Remove drain cover', description: 'Unscrew or pop off the strainer. Clean any visible hair.' },
      { title: 'Plunge it', description: 'Use a flat-bottom plunger with 2–3 inches of water in the shower.' },
      { title: 'Snake the drain', description: 'Insert a 25-ft drain snake and twist clockwise until it grabs the clog.' },
      { title: 'Final flush', description: 'Run hot water for 2 minutes and check flow.' },
    ],
    parts: ['Drain snake', 'Plunger', 'Zip-it tool (optional)'],
    videoUrl: 'https://www.youtube.com/embed/example-clog',
  },
  'ac-not-cooling': {
    title: 'AC Blowing Warm Air',
    category: 'HVAC',
    color: 'hvac',
    summary: 'Classic Texas summer issue — dirty filter or low refrigerant.',
    time: '18 min',
    steps: [
      { title: 'Check air filter', description: 'Replace if dirty (students almost never change them).' },
      { title: 'Check thermostat', description: 'Make sure it’s set to COOL and fan is AUTO.' },
      { title: 'Inspect outdoor unit', description: 'Clear leaves, grass, and debris from the condenser.' },
      { title: 'Test capacitors', description: 'If fan spins but compressor doesn’t, capacitor may be bad.' },
    ],
    parts: ['MERV 8 or 11 filter', 'Run capacitor'],
  },
  // Add more articles here as you create them
};

export default function ArticleDetail() {
  const { slug } = useParams<{ slug: string }>();
  const article = slug ? articles[slug] : null;
  const [logged, setLogged] = useState(false);

  if (!article) {
    return (
      <>
        <Sidebar />
        <main className="lg:ml-72 pt-14 lg:pt-0 min-h-screen bg-background flex items-center justify-center">
          <div className="text-center">
            <h1 className="text-3xl font-bold">Article not found</h1>
            <p className="mt-4">The guide you’re looking for doesn’t exist yet.</p>
            <Button asChild className="mt-6">
              <Link to="/knowledge-base">Back to Knowledge Base</Link>
            </Button>
          </div>
        </main>
      </>
    );
  }

  const logRepair = () => {
    // TODO: Later call Supabase + trigger slack-alert-sender
    setLogged(true);
    setTimeout(() => setLogged(false), 2000);
  };

  return (
    <>
      <Sidebar />
      <main className="lg:ml-72 pt-14 lg:pt-0 min-h-screen bg-background">
        <div className="max-w-4xl mx-auto p-6 lg:p-10">
          {/* Back button */}
          <Link
            to="/knowledge-base"
            className="inline-flex items-center gap-2 text-muted-foreground hover:text-foreground mb-8"
          >
            <ArrowLeft className="h-4 w-4" />
            Back to Knowledge Base
          </Link>

          {/* Header */}
          <div className="flex flex-col lg:flex-row lg:items-start lg:justify-between gap-6 mb-10">
            <div>
              <Badge className={`mb-4 ${article.color}`}>{article.category}</Badge>
              <h1 className="text-4xl lg:text-5xl font-bold tracking-tight leading-tight">
                {article.title}
              </h1>
              <p className="mt-4 text-xl text-muted-foreground max-w-2xl">
                {article.summary}
              </p>
            </div>
            <div className="flex items-center gap-3 text-sm text-muted-foreground whitespace-nowrap">
              <Clock className="h-4 w-4" />
              {article.time} fix
            </div>
          </div>

          {/* Quick Actions */}
          <div className="flex gap-4 mb-12">
            <Button onClick={logRepair} size="lg" className="flex-1 lg:flex-none">
              {logged ? '✅ Logged to History' : 'Log This Repair'}
            </Button>
            <Button variant="outline" size="lg" asChild>
              <a href={article.videoUrl || '#'} target="_blank" rel="noopener">
                <PlayCircle className="mr-2 h-5 w-5" />
                Watch Video
              </a>
            </Button>
          </div>

          {/* Summary Card */}
          <Card className="p-8 mb-12 bg-card">
            <h3 className="font-semibold mb-4">Quick Summary</h3>
            <p className="text-foreground/90 leading-relaxed">{article.summary}</p>
          </Card>

          {/* Step-by-Step */}
          <h2 className="text-2xl font-semibold mb-6">Step-by-Step Repair</h2>
          <Accordion type="single" collapsible className="space-y-4" defaultValue="step-0">
            {article.steps.map((step, index) => (
              <AccordionItem
                key={index}
                value={`step-${index}`}
                className="border border-border rounded-2xl overflow-hidden"
              >
                <AccordionTrigger className="px-8 py-6 text-left">
                  <div className="flex items-center gap-4">
                    <div className="w-8 h-8 rounded-full bg-primary/10 flex items-center justify-center font-mono text-primary">
                      {index + 1}
                    </div>
                    <span className="font-semibold text-lg">{step.title}</span>
                  </div>
                </AccordionTrigger>
                <AccordionContent className="px-8 pb-8 pt-2">
                  <p className="text-foreground/90 leading-relaxed">{step.description}</p>
                  {step.safetyNote && (
                    <div className="mt-6 bg-urgent/10 border-l-4 border-urgent p-4 rounded-r-xl flex gap-3">
                      <AlertTriangle className="h-5 w-5 text-urgent mt-0.5 flex-shrink-0" />
                      <p className="text-sm text-urgent">{step.safetyNote}</p>
                    </div>
                  )}
                </AccordionContent>
              </AccordionItem>
            ))}
          </Accordion>

          {/* Parts Needed */}
          {article.parts && article.parts.length > 0 && (
            <Card className="mt-12 p-8">
              <h3 className="font-semibold mb-5 flex items-center gap-2">
                <Tool className="h-5 w-5" />
                Parts / Tools Needed
              </h3>
              <div className="flex flex-wrap gap-3">
                {article.parts.map((part, i) => (
                  <Badge key={i} variant="secondary" className="text-base py-3 px-6">
                    {part}
                  </Badge>
                ))}
              </div>
            </Card>
          )}

          {/* Related Guides */}
          <div className="mt-16">
            <h3 className="font-semibold mb-4">Related Guides</h3>
            <div className="grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 gap-4">
              {Object.entries(articles)
                .filter(([key]) => key !== slug)
                .slice(0, 2)
                .map(([key, art]) => (
                  <Link key={key} to={`/knowledge-base/${key}`}>
                    <Card className="p-6 hover:bg-muted/50 transition-colors">
                      <Badge className={`mb-3 ${art.color}`}>{art.category}</Badge>
                      <p className="font-medium leading-tight">{art.title}</p>
                    </Card>
                  </Link>
                ))}
            </div>
          </div>
        </div>
      </main>
    </>
  );
}

How to test it right now

  1. Save the file
  2. Go to:
    • /knowledge-base/clogged-drain
    • /knowledge-base/ac-not-cooling (You can add more articles to the articles object anytime)
  3. Click “Log This Repair” → shows temporary success (ready for Supabase later)

Next steps you can do immediately

  • Add more articles to the articles object (takes 30 seconds each)
  • Later replace the hardcoded object with a Supabase query (useQuery + articles table)

What do you want next? Just reply with the name or number:

  1. Video Library page (grid of training videos)
  2. Mobile bottom navigation (for phone-only use)
  3. Save diagnostic log (connects to Supabase + triggers your Slack alert)
  4. Theme toggle in the sidebar
  5. Profile page polish

Or say “add more articles” and I’ll give you 8 more ready-to-paste ones.

We’re building something your techs are actually going to use every single day. This thing is coming together fast! 🛠️

Your move — what’s next?

add more articles

✅ Done! I added 10 new high-quality articles (total now 12) specifically for student housing repairs. All use your category colors, include realistic steps, safety notes, parts lists, and estimated fix times.

How to update

  1. Open src/pages/ArticleDetail.tsx
  2. Replace the entire articles object (lines ~30–80 in the version I gave you) with the new one below.
tsx
// Replace your old articles object with this full version:
const articles: Record<string, Article> = {
  'clogged-drain': {
    title: 'Shower Drain Completely Clogged',
    category: 'Plumbing',
    color: 'plumbing',
    summary: 'Most common call in student housing. Usually hair + soap buildup in the P-trap.',
    time: '12 min',
    steps: [
      { title: 'Safety first', description: 'Turn off water at the shower valve.' },
      { title: 'Remove drain cover', description: 'Unscrew or pop off the strainer. Clean visible hair.' },
      { title: 'Plunge it', description: 'Use a flat-bottom plunger with 2–3 inches of water.' },
      { title: 'Snake the drain', description: 'Insert a 25-ft drain snake and twist clockwise.' },
      { title: 'Final flush', description: 'Run hot water for 2 minutes and check flow.' },
    ],
    parts: ['Drain snake', 'Plunger', 'Zip-it tool'],
  },

  'ac-not-cooling': {
    title: 'AC Blowing Warm Air',
    category: 'HVAC',
    color: 'hvac',
    summary: 'Classic Texas summer issue — dirty filter or low refrigerant.',
    time: '18 min',
    steps: [
      { title: 'Check air filter', description: 'Replace if dirty (students almost never change them).' },
      { title: 'Check thermostat', description: 'Make sure it’s set to COOL and fan is AUTO.' },
      { title: 'Inspect outdoor unit', description: 'Clear debris from the condenser.' },
      { title: 'Test capacitors', description: 'If fan spins but compressor doesn’t, capacitor may be bad.' },
    ],
    parts: ['MERV 8 or 11 filter', 'Run capacitor'],
  },

  'toilet-running': {
    title: 'Toilet Keeps Running (Never Stops Filling)',
    category: 'Plumbing',
    color: 'plumbing',
    summary: 'Worn flapper or faulty fill valve — very common after student parties.',
    time: '8 min',
    steps: [
      { title: 'Check flapper', description: 'Lift the lid and press down on the flapper. If it stops, replace it.' },
      { title: 'Adjust chain', description: 'Shorten the chain so it doesn’t get caught.' },
      { title: 'Replace fill valve', description: 'If still running, swap the entire fill valve (takes 5 minutes).' },
    ],
    parts: ['New flapper', 'Fill valve kit'],
  },

  'no-hot-water': {
    title: 'No Hot Water in Apartment',
    category: 'Plumbing',
    color: 'plumbing',
    summary: 'Water heater pilot light out or tripped breaker — happens constantly in student units.',
    time: '15 min',
    steps: [
      { title: 'Check breaker', description: 'Reset the water heater breaker in the panel.' },
      { title: 'Relight pilot', description: 'Follow the label on the tank (gas units only).' },
      { title: 'Check thermostat', description: 'Set to 120°F and wait 30 minutes.' },
    ],
    parts: ['Match or lighter (for pilot)'],
    videoUrl: 'https://www.youtube.com/embed/example-hot-water',
  },

  'garbage-disposal-jammed': {
    title: 'Garbage Disposal Jammed / Humming',
    category: 'Appliances',
    color: 'appliance',
    summary: 'Students love to put everything down it. Quick fix 90% of the time.',
    time: '6 min',
    steps: [
      { title: 'Safety first', description: 'Turn off breaker or unplug unit.' },
      { title: 'Use Allen wrench', description: 'Insert hex key into bottom and turn back and forth.' },
      { title: 'Reset button', description: 'Press the red reset button on the bottom.' },
    ],
    parts: ['Allen wrench (usually comes with unit)'],
  },

  'gfci-tripping': {
    title: 'GFCI Outlet Keeps Tripping',
    category: 'Electrical',
    color: 'electrical',
    summary: 'Common in bathrooms and kitchens — moisture or faulty appliance.',
    time: '10 min',
    steps: [
      { title: 'Reset GFCI', description: 'Press RESET on the outlet.' },
      { title: 'Unplug everything', description: 'Remove all devices and test again.' },
      { title: 'Check for water', description: 'Dry area completely and test.' },
    ],
    parts: ['New GFCI outlet (if needed)'],
  },

  'low-water-pressure': {
    title: 'Low Water Pressure in Unit',
    category: 'Plumbing',
    color: 'plumbing',
    summary: 'Aerator clogged or shut-off valve partially closed.',
    time: '7 min',
    steps: [
      { title: 'Check shut-off valves', description: 'Under sink — make sure fully open.' },
      { title: 'Clean aerator', description: 'Unscrew tip of faucet and clean screen.' },
      { title: 'Check aerator in showerhead', description: 'Same process.' },
    ],
    parts: ['Vinegar (for cleaning)'],
  },

  'refrigerator-not-cooling': {
    title: 'Refrigerator Not Cooling',
    category: 'Appliances',
    color: 'appliance',
    summary: 'Dirty coils or frozen evaporator fan — huge issue in student fridges.',
    time: '22 min',
    steps: [
      { title: 'Check temperature setting', description: 'Set to 37°F fridge / 0°F freezer.' },
      { title: 'Clean condenser coils', description: 'Pull fridge out and vacuum coils underneath.' },
      { title: 'Check evaporator fan', description: 'Listen for fan in freezer — if silent, may need replacement.' },
    ],
    parts: ['Coil brush'],
  },

  'washer-leaking': {
    title: 'Washer Leaking from Bottom',
    category: 'Appliances',
    color: 'appliance',
    summary: 'Worn drain hose or bad door boot seal.',
    time: '14 min',
    steps: [
      { title: 'Check hoses', description: 'Tighten both supply and drain hoses.' },
      { title: 'Level the machine', description: 'Adjust front feet so it’s perfectly level.' },
      { title: 'Inspect door seal', description: 'Look for tears or debris in the rubber boot.' },
    ],
    parts: ['New drain hose (if cracked)'],
  },

  'flickering-lights': {
    title: 'Lights Flickering or Buzzing',
    category: 'Electrical',
    color: 'electrical',
    summary: 'Loose bulb, bad dimmer, or loose wiring — common in older student units.',
    time: '9 min',
    steps: [
      { title: 'Tighten bulb', description: 'Screw bulb all the way in.' },
      { title: 'Try different bulb', description: 'LEDs can flicker on old dimmers.' },
      { title: 'Check connections', description: 'Turn off breaker and tighten wires at fixture.' },
    ],
    parts: ['New LED bulb'],
  },

  'thermostat-not-working': {
    title: 'Thermostat Not Responding',
    category: 'HVAC',
    color: 'hvac',
    summary: 'Dead batteries or lost Wi-Fi — students change settings constantly.',
    time: '5 min',
    steps: [
      { title: 'Replace batteries', description: 'Most units take 2 AA batteries.' },
      { title: 'Check breaker', description: 'Reset HVAC breaker if display is blank.' },
      { title: 'Reboot unit', description: 'Turn off breaker for 30 seconds.' },
    ],
    parts: ['AA batteries'],
  },

  'frozen-pipe': {
    title: 'Frozen or Burst Pipe (Winter)',
    category: 'Plumbing',
    color: 'urgent',
    summary: 'Emergency — shut off water immediately.',
    time: '25 min',
    steps: [
      { title: 'Shut off main water', description: 'Find main shutoff and turn clockwise.' },
      { title: 'Thaw pipe', description: 'Use hair dryer on low or warm towels (never open flame).' },
      { title: 'Call if burst', description: 'If water is spraying, call maintenance immediately.' },
    ],
    parts: ['Pipe insulation (preventive)'],
    videoUrl: 'https://www.youtube.com/embed/example-frozen-pipe',
  },
};
